title: Adjoint-state Gradient Sanity Checks
summary: Adjoint-state gradients make large-scale FWI practical, but implementation errors should be caught with deterministic gradient checks before experiment scaling.
assumptions: forward and adjoint wavefields use compatible discretization choices
the dry-run plan records expected gradient and loss artifacts before execution exists

parameter_advice.gradient_check: Run a finite-difference directional derivative check on a tiny synthetic case before trusting large inversion gradients.
parameter_advice.time_step: Keep the time step consistent with the forward solver stability limit before interpreting gradient instability.
parameter_advice.regularization: Separate numerical gradient bugs from regularization choices by checking an unregularized tiny case first.
